name: Russ Morgan
description: American big band leader and arranger, songwriter (1904–1969)
abstract: Russell Morgan (April 29, 1904 – August 7, 1969) was an American big band leader and arranger during the 1930s and 1940s. He was best known for being one of the composers of the song "You're Nobody till Somebody Loves You", with Larry Stock and James Cavanaugh, and was the first to record it in 1944.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I had played with him at one or two jobs when the Sirens put on some extra men and he was playing, he and Fuzzy Farrar at the Armory, Scranton Armory, so and besides I knew him when he played a couple of jobs around Wilkes Barre

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: I got a job for him with Russ Morgan's band... Russ Morgan, Russ Morgan needed a drummer. So, I said, I recommend it to him.

about_quotes.speaker: Steve "Ted" Brown
about_quotes.evidence: And this Russ Morgan, he wrote an article some time back in the Post. He'd write practically everything about me...

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: And but anyway there was a Russ Morgan up there shaking the stick, not playing his trombone
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: sometimes Russ Morgan that's him yeah Russ Morgan did most of it he was the big arranger
